canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, IconQA is consistently characterized as a dataset for visual question answering (VQA). The specific focus of this dataset is on 'diagram understanding', a task described as a form of VQA. In practice, IconQA is employed as a downstream dataset for fine-tuning Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs). It is used to evaluate visual reasoning capabilities and is sometimes studied alongside other VQA datasets such as ScienceQA. One source also identifies IconQA as a component task within the CMIT benchmark.
papers: Be Confident: Uncovering Overfitting in MLLM Multi-Task Tuning
Dynamic Mixture of Curriculum LoRA Experts for Continual Multimodal Instruction Tuning
Learn from Downstream and Be Yourself in Multimodal Large Language Models Fine-Tuning

definitions.text: A visual question answering dataset for diagram understanding used as one task in the CMIT benchmark.
definitions.paper: Dynamic Mixture of Curriculum LoRA Experts for Continual Multimodal Instruction Tuning
